length_sanity.detail: 2980 words is 0.7% below the standard floor of 3000 — well inside the 30% tolerance (hard floor 2100). Marginal shortfall caused by the cycle-1 revision trimming the line-93 sentence; pass.

topic_scope.detail: Cycle-1 scope_creep finding RESOLVED: line 93 is now the bare-name-plus-pointer form ('You'll meet machine learning properly in Week 3.') — no defining clause, no bolding, exactly the mandated mention-and-defer form. Strict week-3 mechanism sweep: zero occurrences of training/model/weights/features/classifier/dataset/neural network/deep learning/LLM/GenAI vocabulary. The rule-from-repetition mechanism ('How repeated data becomes a rule') and the held-back-case test (Implementation step 4) are taught generically, in everyday terms, squarely inside the headline 'how machines find rules in repeated data' — no ML technique is named or defined. 'Learned' appears only in colloquial everyday use (lines 132, 167), not as a technical term. No assessment references anywhere in the corpus.

beginner_readability.detail: Every new term bolded and plain-defined on first use (pattern, pattern recognition, generalisation, overgeneralisation), concrete example before abstraction throughout (2-4-6-8 sequence, chai stall, junk-mail pile). No paragraph introduces 3+ new terms. Avg sentence length 15.4 words across Introduction + Core Concepts (109 sentences); 0 hedging phrases. 0 distinct soft findings — no escalation.

verdict: PASS — cycle-2 revision resolves the single cycle-1 hard finding: the line-93 'machine learning' mention is now the bare-name-plus-Week-3-pointer form with no defining clause, satisfying scope_discipline's mention-and-defer rule. Strict mechanism sweep confirms zero ML/week-3 vocabulary leakage (no training/model/weights/neural/LLM terms; mechanism taught generically within the headline 'how machines find rules in repeated data'). No assessment references. Zero forward references; readability clean (avg 15.4 w/sentence, 0 hedges, all new terms defined on first use). One soft finding carries forward: low_grounding (narrative_seed_only, zero citations — expected for this mode) for HITL #3/#4 visibility.
